rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): hide '/ for commands' hint in agent windows
rawCommits.body:
Floating desktop agent windows can be very narrow, so the input info
bar wraps and the orbit/environment indicators get pushed onto a
second line. Add a `compact` flag that's threaded ChatPanel ->
ChatInputBar and set on the chat surface inside AgentChatWindowPanel
to suppress the chat-mode commands hint there. The image/3d-mode
labels are kept since they convey active state, and the main chat
surface is unaffected.

rawCommits.subject: feat(image-gen): default to gpt-image-2 and expose generate_image tool
rawCommits.body:
Promotes gpt-image-2 to the first entry in IMAGE_MODELS (so the chat
input picker and the aura3d Image Generation app both default to it),
and adds a provider-less `generate_image` workspace tool so chat agents
can fulfil natural-language requests like "create an image of a cat"
without the user having to invoke the `/image` slash command.

The new tool routes through aura-os-server, drains the upstream
aura-router SSE stream, and returns the final `completed` payload as a
single JSON value shaped for `ImageBlock` (imageUrl, originalUrl, prompt,
model, meta). Includes mock-router unit tests covering the happy path,
upstream `error` events, and missing `completed` events.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: default to gpt-image-2 and expose generate_image tool

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): keep middle panel mounted across app switches
rawCommits.body:
Lift the visible `ResponsiveMainLane` into `DesktopShell.mainPanelHost`
so the middle root panel survives app switches. Each app's `MainPanel`
now returns inner content only — the shell wraps everything in a
persistent lane that retains DOM identity across navigations, which
eliminates the size-snap flash where the wallpaper briefly showed
through before the new app's content rendered.

The new `bareMainPanel` flag on `AuraApp` lets the Desktop app keep its
transparent wallpaper-bleed surface (no lane chrome).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep middle panel mounted across app switches

rawCommits.subject: fix(interface): show loop runs in Run pane regardless of viewed chat
rawCommits.body:
The Run sidekick was filtering its task list by the URL's
`agentInstanceId`, but that param is the chat agent the user is viewing
while the loop runs on a separate `Loop`-role instance. Every
`task_started` row (and every `/loop/status` rehydration) carries the
loop instance id, so the chat-scoped filter dropped them all and the
pane stayed on "No tasks" the moment Run was pressed.

The Run pane is project-scoped UI, so query the panel store by project
only and let row-level project filtering handle isolation.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): accept passing tests as task-completion proof
rawCommits.body:
Adds a 'tests-as-truth' completion path so a CompletionContract
failure (task_done with no file edits) is bridged to Done when the
event stream contains at least one successful test runner invocation
(cargo test, pnpm test, vitest, pytest, go test, ...). The dev_loop
side-effect pipeline now records TestPassEvidence per task, the
reconciler grows a MarkDone(TestEvidenceAccepted) verdict, and the
server suppresses the synthetic task_failed in favor of a synthetic
task_completed carrying the test_pass_evidence payload. The interface
event types and task-status store are updated to surface the new
outcome, and dev_loop_dod_regression covers the detector + reconciler
override + harness-owns-DoD deference for empty-path writes.

Drive-by: re-export CACHE_ENTRY_MAX_AGE under cfg(test) and fix the
auth_guard tests import so 'cargo test --lib' compiles after the
prior refactor that moved get_cached_session into the cache submodule.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: accept passing tests as task-completion proof

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 0 — harness_agent_id helper, session tracing, turn_in_progress remap
rawCommits.body:
Foundation for partitioning the upstream harness `agent_id` per
AgentInstance. Adds:
- `aura_os_core::harness_agent_id(template, instance)` helper, the
  single source of truth for the `{template}::{instance}` partition
  format used in upcoming phases.
- `tracing::info!` on every harness session open with the partition
  key, billing ids, so we can prove from logs which sessions share
  turn-locks.
- `remap_harness_error_to_api` in chat::errors that recognizes the
  raw "turn is currently in progress" upstream string (and the
  `turn_in_progress` code) and surfaces it as a structured
  `ApiError::agent_busy` instead of leaking the harness wording.

No behavior change: callers still pass the bare template id for
`SessionConfig.agent_id`. Phase 1 will start using the partition
helper at the call sites.

Per the spec's "simpler scope option", the helper lives in
chat::errors only; the swarm HTTP path keeps its anyhow-string
error today and gets a TODO(phase 0.5) breadcrumb so a follow-up
patch can converge those paths onto a structured wire shape.

Refs: c:\Users\n3o\.cursor\plans\robust-concurrent-agent-infra_74e43e47.plan.md
Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 0 — harness_agent_id helper, session tracing, turn_in_progress remap

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 1a - add template_agent_id to SessionInit/SessionConfig
rawCommits.body:
Extends the harness wire protocol with an optional `template_agent_id`
field on `SessionInit` and `SessionConfig`. Phase 1b/1c will start
setting it: `agent_id` becomes the partitioned `{template}::{instance}`
key (the harness turn-lock), `template_agent_id` carries the stable
template id for skill / permissions / billing lookup.

- Adds `SessionInit::template_agent_id: Option<String>` (skip_serializing_if).
- Adds `SessionConfig::template_agent_id: Option<String>` and
  threads it through `build_session_init`.
- Adds `template_agent_id` to the swarm `POST /v1/agents` body so
  the gateway can persist it alongside the partition agent record.
- Replaces the Phase-0 TODO in `session.rs` tracing with the now-
  available `template_agent_id` field.

No behavior change: every caller still leaves `template_agent_id`
as `None` and `agent_id` as the bare template, so traffic is
identical to pre-Phase-0. Phase 1b switches the call sites.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 1a - add template_agent_id to SessionInit/SessionConfig

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 1b — partition harness agent_id at chat call sites
rawCommits.body:
Activates the partitioning helper at every chat-side `SessionConfig`
construction. Each `AgentInstance` now gets its own upstream harness
turn-lock by setting `agent_id = harness_agent_id(template, Some(instance))`
and preserving the template id in `template_agent_id` for skill /
permissions / billing lookup.

- `instance_route.rs`: partition key for both `SessionConfig.agent_id`
  and the in-memory `chat_sessions` registry key. Two AgentInstances
  of the same template no longer collide upstream.
- `agent_route.rs` (legacy bare-agent chat): uses `{template}::default`
  partition so it sits on a stable separate slot from any project-
  bound instance.
- `runtime.rs` smoke test: same pattern.
- `setup.rs::reset_{agent,instance}_session`: rebuild the in-memory
  `session_key` with the same `harness_agent_id` form the chat routes
  store under, otherwise reset would silently fail to evict the live
  session. The instance variant resolves the parent template via
  `agent_instance_service.get_instance` to compute the partition.

Consumer impact on the `chat_sessions` registry:

- `state::ChatSession` now carries both `agent_id` (the partition
  string, opaque) and a new `template_agent_id` (the stable Aura
  template id). The doc-comment on `ChatSession::agent_id` is updated
  to spell out the partition semantics.
- `streaming.rs::insert_delegated_chat_session` threads
  `SessionConfig::template_agent_id` through to the new field.
- `crud::update::invalidate_chat_sessions_for_agent` switches its
  ownership filter from `session.agent_id == template_id` to
  `session.template_agent_id == template_id`. Without this, a
  permissions toggle would no longer evict any live session because
  every key now contains the partition suffix. Doc-comment updated to
  match.

Billing header preservation:

- `aura-os-harness::build_session_init` now derives `aura_agent_id`
  (the X-Aura-Agent-Id billing header) from `template_agent_id` first
  and falls back to `agent_id`. Pre-Phase-1b callers leave
  `template_agent_id = None` and still ship the bare template (no
  behavior change). Phase-1b chat call sites ship the template id, so
  billing keeps aggregating per agent rather than per partition.

Out of scope (deferred):

- The automaton / dev-loop adapter (`start_loop` / `run_single_task`)
  still passes the bare template id — Phase 1c partitions those.
- `projects_helpers::session::project_tool_session_config` is the
  spec-gen / task-extraction tool-session path; left alone for the
  same Phase 1c sweep.

Verification:

- `cargo check -p aura-os-server` clean.
- `cargo check -p aura-os-harness` clean.
- `cargo test -p aura-os-server --lib`: 267 passed.
- `cargo test -p aura-os-harness --lib`: 25 passed.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 1b — partition harness agent_id at chat call sites

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 1c - partition harness agent_id at automaton + project-tool sites
rawCommits.body:
Completes the per-AgentInstance partitioning. The automaton path
(start_loop, run_single_task) and the project-tool session helper
now send `harness_agent_id(template, Some(instance))` as the upstream
`agent_id`, and `template_agent_id` carries the stable template id
for skill / permissions / billing lookup.

- `AutomatonStartParams` gains `agent_id: Option<String>` and
  `template_agent_id: Option<String>` (additive wire fields, both
  `skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none`, so pre-Phase-1c harnesses
  - which derive `agent_id` from the URL path - keep accepting the
  payload until they ship structured-body support).
- `start_loop`: partition derived from `(template, agent_instance_id)`.
- `run_single_task`: partition derived from
  `(template, ephemeral_instance_id)`, so concurrent task runs of
  the same template are now genuinely independent at the harness
  turn-lock layer (matching the Aura-side ephemeral-instance model).
- `project_tool_session_config`: the swarm-mode `remote_instance`
  branch now sends `harness_agent_id(instance.agent_id,
  Some(instance.agent_instance_id))` and `template_agent_id =
  instance.agent_id`. The local-mode synthetic id
  (`{tool_agent_name}-{project_id}`) is left bare on purpose - it is
  already per-project, isn't a real Aura template, and the local
  harness doesn't need partitioning here. Documented inline.
- `ActiveAutomaton`: no harness-bound `agent_id` field exists
  (per-instance harness control flows via `automaton_id` on the
  pre-built `harness_base_url` client), so step 6 of the plan is a
  no-op for this struct.

Sanity grep `agent_id:\s*Some\(.*\.agent_id\.` over
crates/aura-os-harness/src and apps/aura-os-server/src reports only:
- `template_agent_id: Some(...agent_id.to_string())` lines (correct
  template-id placement on the new field), and
- `aura_os_storage::CreateSessionEventRequest.agent_id:
  Some(ctx.project_agent_id.clone())` in chat persist - this is the
  storage event-row `agent_id`, not a harness session/start param,
  so it is intentionally left untouched.

After this phase, every outbound `agent_id` to the upstream harness
is a partition. Phase 2 surfaces a structured agent_busy on the
legacy chat route, and Phase 3 adds intra-partition serialization.

Verification:

- `cargo check -p aura-os-harness` clean.
- `cargo check -p aura-os-server` clean.
- `cargo test -p aura-os-harness --lib`: 25 passed.
- `cargo test -p aura-os-server --lib`: 267 passed.
- `cargo check -p aura-os-server --tests` clean (only pre-existing
  unused-import warnings unrelated to this phase).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 1c - partition harness agent_id at automaton + project-tool sites

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 2 — shared busy guard + SSE harness-error remap
rawCommits.body:
Closes the two remaining leak paths for the raw upstream
"A turn is currently in progress; send cancel first" string after
Phase 1 partitioned the harness `agent_id`:

- Extracts the chat-vs-automation conflict guard into
  `chat::busy::reject_if_partition_busy`. The instance route keeps
  its strict `(project_id, instance)` check; the legacy bare-agent
  route now scans `automaton_registry` by `template_agent_id` and
  refuses with `ApiError::agent_busy` when any of its instances are
  running an automaton.
- `ActiveAutomaton` gains a `template_agent_id` field so the cross-
  partition check is O(N) without async lookups under the registry
  lock; populated by every `automaton_registry.insert(...)` site
  (start_loop, run_single_task).
- SSE forwarder (`harness_broadcast_to_sse` / `harness_event_to_sse`)
  intercepts `HarnessOutbound::Error` events whose code/message
  matches the harness turn-in-progress pattern and remaps them to a
  structured `agent_busy` SSE event before closing the stream, so
  the UI never sees the raw harness wording.
- Adds unit tests for the new busy guard.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 2 — shared busy guard + SSE harness-error remap

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 3 — per-partition turn slot with queued SSE hint
rawCommits.body:
Serializes user-message sends on a single ChatSession partition so
back-to-back chat turns can no longer race the upstream harness
turn-lock and trigger the "turn in progress" error mid-stream.

- `ChatSession.turn_slot: Arc<Mutex<()>>` is held for the duration
  of one user-message turn. Released by a per-turn sentinel task
  that watches the harness broadcast for `AssistantMessageEnd` or
  `Error` (the same set used as `should_close` in
  `harness_broadcast_to_sse`).
- Queue bound: at most one waiter pending. A third concurrent send
  on the same partition surfaces `ApiError::agent_busy` with a
  "queue full" reason instead of stacking unbounded.
- SSE prefix gains a `progress: queued` event when the call was
  queued, so the UI can render "Queued behind current turn"
  distinct from the existing `progress: connecting`. Cross-partition
  traffic is unaffected and still runs in parallel.
- Adds `chat::turn_slot` module with tests for slot acquisition and
  release on terminal events / broadcast close.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 3 — per-partition turn slot with queued SSE hint

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 4 — frontend agent-busy uniformity + queued progress hint
rawCommits.body:
Surfaces the Phase 2 / Phase 3 server-side improvements to the UI:

- `useAgentBusy`: detects `code: "agent_busy"` uniformly across the
  bare-agent and instance chat routes (Phase 2), exposes
  `automaton_id` so the consumer can render a "Stop the loop to
  chat" affordance, and distinguishes a "queue full" reason from
  the automation-running reason for accurate copy.
- `api/client.ts::isAgentBusyError`: returns structured info instead
  of a boolean and adds the harness raw-string fallback so older
  servers without the SSE remap still surface a clean error.
- `ChatInputBar` (and the SSE consumer): handles
  `progress { stage: "queued" }` with an inline "Queued behind
  current turn…" hint distinct from the busy spinner; clears as
  soon as the first text/tool delta arrives.
- `harness-protocol.ts`: mirrors the optional `template_agent_id`
  field added to `SessionInit` in Phase 1a so the hand-maintained
  TS shape matches the Rust wire.

Tests added for the new hook signals and the queued-state UI.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 4 — frontend agent-busy uniformity + queued progress hint

rawCommits.subject: test(concurrency): phase 5 - concurrent-agents integration + stress tests + role doc refresh
rawCommits.body:
Validates the Phase 1-3 partitioning contract end-to-end:

- `concurrent_agents.rs` integration tests:
  - `concurrent_chat_distinct_agents` - two templates run interleaved.
  - `concurrent_chat_same_agent_multi_instance` - two instances of
    one template send distinct `agent_id` partitions and interleave.
  - `chat_during_automation_isolated_instance` - chat on instance B
    succeeds while instance A is automating; chat on instance A
    refuses with `agent_busy { automaton_id }`.
  - `same_partition_second_turn_queues` - second send emits the
    Phase-3 `progress: queued` SSE prefix before its first delta.
  - `same_partition_third_turn_rejects` - third concurrent send
    surfaces `agent_busy` with the queue-full reason.
- `concurrent_agents_stress_test.rs` (`#[ignore]`-gated): 32 parallel
  chat partitions, asserts wall-clock <= 2x single-stream duration
  and zero `turn_in_progress` / `queue_full` observed. Run with
  `cargo test --release --test concurrent_agents_stress_test
  -- --include-ignored`.
- Test scaffold: `aura_os_harness::test_support::FakeHarness`
  records every `SessionInit` it sees and replays scripted
  `OutboundMessage` streams on configurable delays, so the tests
  assert directly on partition `agent_id`s and timing.
- `AgentInstanceRole` doc-comment in `aura-os-core::enums` rewritten
  to describe the post-Phase-3 reality (each role gets its own
  partition via `harness_agent_id`) instead of the in-progress
  language from before.

Also adds a `streaming::tests::harness_turn_in_progress_remapped_to_agent_busy_sse_event`
regression test that drives `harness_broadcast_to_sse` with a
`turn_in_progress` error and asserts the SSE output is a clean
`agent_busy` event with no leakage of the raw harness wording.

Tooling:
- `aura-os-harness` gains a `test-support` Cargo feature gating
  `FakeHarness` for downstream consumers; the server crate opts in
  via its `[dev-dependencies]`.
- `BusyMatch`, `evaluate_partition_busy`, `acquire_turn_slot`,
  `TurnSlotAcquired/Guard/QueueFull`, `MAX_PENDING_TURNS`, and
  `harness_broadcast_to_sse` are promoted from `pub(super)` /
  `pub(crate)` to `pub` and re-exported through the existing
  `handlers::agents::chat_pub` and `handlers_test_support` seams.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 5 - concurrent-agents integration + stress tests + role doc refresh

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): phase 6 — configurable WS slot cap + clean capacity-exhausted error
rawCommits.body:
Closes the last operational gap from the partitioning plan: the
upstream `aura-node` WS slot cap is now configurable end-to-end
and exhaustion surfaces a structured error instead of a raw 503.

- `AURA_HARNESS_WS_SLOTS` env var (default 128) controls the
  upstream concurrent-session cap. Read on the server via
  `app_builder::parse_harness_ws_slots`, exported on `AppState`,
  and forwarded to the autospawned local harness child process
  so the value matches end-to-end. Zero / non-numeric values
  warn-and-default.
- New `aura_os_harness::HarnessError::CapacityExhausted` variant
  detects upstream 503 / WS close 1013 (Try Again Later) at
  session-open time in both `swarm_harness::parse_create_session_response`
  (matches structured `code: capacity_exhausted` JSON or opaque
  503 bodies) and `local_harness::open_session` (matches
  `tungstenite::Error::Http` 503 and 1013 close-frame display
  strings). Wrapped via `anyhow::Error::new` so callers recover
  the typed cause via `downcast_ref`.
- New `SessionBridgeError::CapacityExhausted(String)` propagates
  the typed cause through `SessionBridge::open_and_send_user_message`
  for chat / handler call sites.
- New `ApiError::harness_capacity_exhausted(cap)` returns a 503
  with `code: "harness_capacity_exhausted"`, structured
  `{ configured_cap, retry_after_seconds }` data, and a clear
  user-visible message. Wired into every harness session-open
  call site:
    * `chat::streaming::open_harness_chat_stream` (typed bridge
      error -> `map_session_bridge_error`).
    * `handlers::agents::runtime::test_agent_runtime`,
      `handlers::specs::gen` and `handlers::tasks::extraction`
      (anyhow::Error -> `chat::errors::map_harness_error_to_api`
       with a context-specific fallback closure).
    * `handlers::dev_loop::start::map_start_error` /
      `handlers::dev_loop::adapter::{start_loop, run_single_task}`
      via `AutomatonStartError::Response { status: 503 }` heuristic.
- `FakeHarness::set_capacity_limit` lets integration tests drive
  the cap-exhausted path without a real upstream harness.
- Frontend: new `isHarnessCapacityExhaustedError` predicate parses
  the structured 503 payload; `useAgentBusy` surfaces a
  `server_busy` reason with `retryAfterSeconds`; the SSE error
  handler classifies it as `harnessCapacityExhaustedError` and
  renders "Server is busy — try again in N seconds." instead of
  leaking the raw 503.
- Tests: env-var parsing (default / blank / zero / non-numeric /
  valid), `ApiError::harness_capacity_exhausted` shape,
  `map_harness_error_to_api` capacity vs fallback,
  `map_session_bridge_error` capacity vs send, and an end-to-end
  `FakeHarness` integration test that verifies the bridge
  surfaces `SessionBridgeError::CapacityExhausted` once the cap
  is hit.

FOLLOW-UP: the upstream `aura-node` harness binary is expected
to read `AURA_HARNESS_WS_SLOTS` to size its semaphore. If the
build in use does not yet honour the env var, the server's view
of the cap stays consistent for operator messaging, but the
actual upstream limit may stay at the harness default until that
side is patched. We do not own that source here.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: phase 6 — configurable WS slot cap + clean capacity-exhausted error

rawCommits.subject: feat(concurrency): rollout flag — AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS gates harness_agent_id partitioning
rawCommits.body:
Adds a single env var that operators flip to opt out of Phase-1
partitioning when their harness deployment hasn't yet shipped
matching support, leaving every other Phase-0-6 improvement
(busy guard, SSE error remap, queued turn slot, capacity-exhausted
mapping) intact.

- `AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS` (default `true`) is parsed at startup
  with case-insensitive `true|false|yes|no|1|0` semantics, logged
  at INFO, and exposed on `AppState.partition_agent_ids`.
- `aura_os_core::harness_agent_id_gated(enabled, template, instance)`
  returns the partition id when enabled, the bare template id when
  not. Every chat / automaton / project-tool call site now reads
  through this helper, so flipping the flag flips the upstream
  `agent_id` shape end-to-end.
- `template_agent_id` is set to `Some(template)` regardless of the
  flag — harmless redundancy when off, keeps the wire stable when
  the flag is flipped on.
- Adds an integration-test variant that verifies flag-off behavior
  (both instances share the bare template id, partitioning
  disabled) and confirms the existing flag-on tests still hold.

After this lands, the recommended rollout is:
1. Deploy server with `AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS=false`.
2. Deploy the matching harness build.
3. Flip the flag to `true` (or unset for the default).
4. Watch logs / metrics; revert by setting back to `false` if needed.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: rollout flag — AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS gates harness_agent_id partitioning

rawCommits.subject: refactor(concurrency): remove AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS flag — integrate partitioning unconditionally
rawCommits.body:
The partitioning helper is wire-backward-compatible by construction:
old harnesses see `{template}::{instance}` as a distinct agent_id
and turn-lock it separately, and the optional `template_agent_id`
field is ignored where unsupported. There is no rollout risk to
leaving partitioning always-on, so the flag is dead complexity.

- Drops `AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS` env var, its parser, and the
  startup log line.
- Drops `AppState.partition_agent_ids` and removes the flag from
  every test fixture.
- Drops `aura_os_core::harness_agent_id_gated`; every call site
  now uses `harness_agent_id(template, instance)` directly.
- Drops the flag-off integration test variants — flag-on
  partitioning is already covered by
  `concurrent_chat_same_agent_multi_instance` and friends.
- Removes the partition-agent-ids rollout runbook.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remove AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS flag — integrate partitioning unconditionally

rawCommits.subject: feat(chat): hoist [auto-build] / [task_done test gate] prose markers into CommandBlocks
rawCommits.body:
The expander in `tool-markers.ts` only matched the legacy
`[tool: NAME(arg) -> ok|error]` shape, so prose markers like
`[auto-build: cargo check --workspace --tests]` and
`[task_done test gate: PASSED in 3761ms - 173 passed, 0 failed, 3 skipped]`
fell through to `SegmentedContent` and rendered as raw markdown
instead of collapsible Block cards.

Add a second regex for the `[<gate>: <body>]` pseudo-tool shape
(`auto-build`, `task_done test gate`) and emit them as synthetic
`run_command` entries so the existing CommandBlock renderer picks
them up. PASSED/FAILED bodies set `result`/`isError`. A pairing
pass merges consecutive announcement + result markers from the same
gate into one card so each gate renders as a single block instead
of two stacked stubs.

Streaming-tail trimming in `trimIncompleteToolMarkerTail` now also
recognizes incomplete pseudo-tool prefixes so half-typed
`[auto-build: cargo che` doesn't flicker before the closing `]`
arrives.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hoist [auto-build] / [task_done test gate] prose markers into CommandBlocks

rendered.title: Concurrent agents, mobile iOS, and a unified Aura model proxy
rendered.intro: A heavy day across the stack: chat agents now run concurrently without colliding on harness turn-locks, iOS gets a proper first-class mobile shell isolated from desktop, model traffic consolidates onto the Aura proxy, and a long thread of reliability work hardens harness sidecar startup, capacity exhaustion, and dev-loop completion.

entries.title: Recoverable spec drafts on model timeout
entries.summary: Streamed spec markdown is preserved when create_spec is interrupted, and agent stream output gains better server-side observability.

items.text: Timed-out create_spec runs no longer discard the partially streamed markdown — drafts stay recoverable in the interface, and agents are steered away from oversized spec payloads.
Server now logs structured agent stream output summaries across the chat, persistence, and tool dispatch paths to make production debugging less guesswork.

entries.title: Desktop refuses to launch with a broken local harness
entries.summary: The Desktop app now fails closed when the managed local harness sidecar never passes its startup health probe.

items.text: If the local harness sidecar fails its startup health check, Desktop kills the managed process instead of proceeding with a half-broken backend, preventing a class of confusing post-launch errors on Mac, Windows, and Linux.

entries.title: Aura-proxied models, generate_image, and a steadier desktop shell
entries.summary: A wide push consolidates model traffic onto the Aura proxy, ships a built-in image generation tool, and tightens the desktop shell, run pane, and streaming internals.

items.text: All model traffic now routes through the Aura proxy: BYOK org-integration auth and direct provider catalog entries (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, xAI, OpenRouter) are dropped, the chat model picker collapses to a single Aura group, and image-only models are filtered out of the chat picker.

gpt-image-2 becomes the default image model, and a new provider-less generate_image workspace tool lets chat agents fulfill natural-language image requests without the /image slash command, with mock-router tests covering happy path and upstream errors.

The middle panel now stays mounted across app switches via a persistent DesktopShell main lane, eliminating the wallpaper-flash on navigation, while floating agent windows hide the cramped '/ for commands' hint.

Run pane reliability: rows are seeded from the authoritative project task list so reloads or cleared local storage no longer hide completed runs, and loop-instance tasks now appear regardless of the chat agent currently being viewed.
Task output persistence stops repeatedly parsing localStorage during streaming by reusing a single IndexedDB connection and debouncing cache writes, noticeably reducing jank on long runs.

Server hardening: stale auth fallback is capped, sensitive routes fail closed, harness WebSocket commands are now bounded with explicit delivery errors and redacted frame logging, and dev-loop runs receive the same normalized agent permissions as chat sessions.
list_agents is promoted to a first-class agent capability and new agent control callback endpoints land on the server, giving permission UI and orchestration explicit, granular handles.

Large internal restructuring across server, crates, and the interface (auth_guard, app_builder, error mapping, entities, permissions, stream handlers, billing/leaderboard queries) sets up the day's later concurrency and capacity work without changing user-facing behavior.

media.alt: Aura proxy becomes the only path for model traffic
media.caption: The chat model picker is now a single Aura group, with image-only models filtered out of agent chats.

entries.title: Smarter changelog media planner
entries.summary: The internal changelog media-seeding pipeline learns from past misses and stops emitting low-signal candidates.

items.text: Media planner now preserves intended targets, hardens its inference seeding against navigation-contract drift, and broadens surface coverage so generated changelog media maps to the right screens.
A new lessons knowledge file lets the planner learn from historical media misses, and token-only candidates are now skipped to avoid noisy seed plans.
CI re-triggered the desktop sidecar build after the harness ListAgents capability fix to confirm green packaging.

entries.title: iOS becomes a first-class platform with a clean desktop boundary
entries.summary: Aura ships a real mobile iOS experience — dedicated screens, chat input, drawer, and bottom nav — and then isolates it from desktop so neither side regresses.

items.text: First-class iOS views land across project agents, files, tasks, run, process, stats, profile, and settings, with a redesigned MobileChatHeader, MobileChatInputBar, MobileShell, drawer, and bottom nav, all validated against an iPhone simulator signoff matrix.

Mobile code is then extracted into a dedicated interface/src/mobile tree with a mobile-boundary test, a separate MobileSettingsView, and an explicit isMobileLayout route switch — fixing a regression where /projects/settings briefly rendered the mobile screen on desktop and proven by a route-by-route desktop signoff pass.

entries.title: Per-instance harness partitioning ends 'turn in progress' collisions
entries.summary: A multi-phase concurrency overhaul gives each AgentInstance its own upstream harness turn-lock, queues serialized turns cleanly, and surfaces capacity errors as structured states in the UI.

items.text: Each AgentInstance now sends a partitioned {template}::{instance} agent_id upstream, with template_agent_id preserved for skill, permissions, and billing lookups across chat routes, automaton start/run, project-tool sessions, and the in-memory chat session registry — two instances of the same template can finally run in parallel without colliding on the harness turn-lock.

A shared busy guard plus an SSE-level remap converts raw harness 'A turn is currently in progress' strings into a structured agent_busy event, and a per-partition turn slot serializes back-to-back sends with a one-deep queue and a queued progress hint instead of erroring mid-stream.

The chat input now renders 'Queued behind current turn…' distinct from the busy spinner, useAgentBusy detects agent_busy uniformly across both chat routes (with a 'Stop the loop to chat' affordance and queue-full vs automation-running reasons), and harness-protocol TS mirrors the new template_agent_id wire field.

AURA_HARNESS_WS_SLOTS (default 128) makes the upstream concurrent-session cap configurable end-to-end, and capacity exhaustion now surfaces as a structured 503 with retry_after_seconds rendered in the UI as 'Server is busy — try again in N seconds.' instead of a raw 503.

End-to-end concurrent_agents integration tests plus a 32-partition stress test (≤2x single-stream wall clock, zero turn_in_progress observed) validate the contract, backed by a new FakeHarness scaffold that records SessionInits and replays scripted streams.
An AURA_PARTITION_AGENT_IDS rollout flag was briefly added and then removed once the wire turned out to be backward-compatible by construction, leaving partitioning unconditionally on.

Dev-loop completion gains a 'tests-as-truth' path: a CompletionContract failure with no file edits is bridged to Done when the event stream contains a successful test runner invocation (cargo test, pnpm test, vitest, pytest, go test, …), with the synthetic task_failed replaced by a task_completed carrying TestPassEvidence.

Chat now renders [auto-build: …] and [task_done test gate: …] prose markers as collapsible CommandBlock cards (with PASSED/FAILED state) instead of raw markdown, and trims half-typed pseudo-tool prefixes during streaming to avoid flicker.

highlights: Per-instance harness partitioning unblocks truly concurrent agents
First-class iOS mobile views with desktop isolation
All model traffic routes through the Aura proxy
Dev-loop accepts passing tests as completion proof
Configurable WS slot cap with clean capacity-exhausted errors
